EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure moved by to the south during the early morning hours of January 29th bringing a widespread inch and a half to two inches of rain. This resulted in some rivers rising into Minor Flood Stage and several streams overflowing their banks.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rock Creek at Sherrill Drive crested at 7.02' in Minor Flood Stage. Water reached several portions of the Valley Trail between Picnic Areas 7 and 10 in Rock Creek Park.	Water completely covered the Rolling Meadow Trailbridge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Multiple waves of low pressure moved along a frontal boundary bringing several rounds of snow. Two day totals averaged four to six inches. As the system departed, precipitation ended as a light freezing drizzle areawide during the morning of January 16th.
An area of surface low pressure moved by to the south as an upper level disturbance moved through the Ohio Valley into the Northeast. This produced a swath of four to six inches of snow.

A strong area of low pressure tracked from the mid MS River Valley on Jan 9th to the Northeast U.S. Jan 10th. A very strong pressure gradient ahead of the low pressure center resulted in a long period of strong SE winds gusting up to 52 mph that raised water levels to moderate levels at several locations along the Potomac River.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The NOS tide gauge at Washington DC Southwest Waterfront reached a crest of 6.19' MLLW (3.0' MHHW) at 636 PM EST on Jan 9th. Lead time for this event was 12.9 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong onshore flow combined with high astronomical tides due to a New Moon resulted in moderate coastal flooding at Washington DC.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Washington DC Waterfront NOS tide gauge reached a crest of 5.45' at 648 PM EST on March 9th. Lead time for this event was 6.6 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure tracked through the Ohio River Valley and up through the Great Lakes Region. A strong low level jet enhanced moisture flow into the low pressure system leading to an extended period of moderate rain that caused widespread minor flooding with isolated moderate flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The stream gauge on Rock Creek at Sherrill Drive reach minor flood stage with crest of 8.04 feet. At 7 feet, water reaches several portions of the Valley Trail between Picnic Areas 7 and 10 in Rock Creek Park. Water also completely covers the Roiling Meadow Trailbridge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot and humid conditions overspread much of the region on July 15 and July 16, resulting in the hottest temperatures of the Summer in 2024. Heat index values reached and exceeded 100 to 110, with daily record high temperatures tied and set.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport (KDCA) measured a peak heat index value of 108. The high temperature at Washington DC was 102F. Several mesonets in the city measured peak heat indices around 110.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large tree limbs blew down onto power lines near the intersection of 39th Street Northwest and Legation Street Northwest.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A couple of thunderstorms produced sporadic damaging wind gusts and a little hail near the DC and Baltimore metro areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tree damage was reported in Adams Morgan, including a tree that blew down in the 2400 block of 20th Street Northwest.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A hot, humid, and very unstable airmass resulted in scattered pulse thunderstorms that produced several instances of damaging downbursts. A couple instances of wind damage were significant just east of Washington, DC.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Tree damage was reported near Catholic University, including a large tree that blew down near Gallatin Street Northwest and Illinois Avenue Northwest at 9th Street Northwest.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Southeast to south winds pushed water up the tidal Potomac River causing moderate coastal flooding at Washington DC.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Southeast to south winds ahead of a cold front pushed water up the tidal Potomac River causing moderate coastal flooding at DC Waterfront.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Several hail-producing thunderstorms affected the Mid-Atlantic during the afternoon hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter size hail was observed just south of the Thomas Jefferson Memorial.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Persistent SE flow on the east side of slow moving Tropical Storm Debby caused moderate coastal flooding at DC Waterfront.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Persistent SE wind flow on the east side of TS Debby resulted in moderate coastal flooding at DC Waterfront on Aug 9, 2024.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak area of low pressure brought a light wintry mix to the District of Columbia. Snow and sleet accumulations of a coating were observed along with freezing rain accumulations of a trace to a few hundredths.

A prolonged period of onshore flow combined with high astronomical tides resulted in multiple days of moderate coastal flooding at DC Waterfront. Moderate coastal flooding began on Sep 21 and ended on Sep 24, 2024.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate coastal flooding occurred at DC Waterfront. It began on Sep 21 at 9PM and ended that same evening at 1124 PM EST. The crest height was 5.62' and crest time was 1024 PM EST. This was a missed event with 0 lead time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A prolonged period of onshore flow combined with high astronomical tides resulted in multiple days of moderate coastal flooding at DC Waterfront. Moderate coastal flooding began on Sep 21 and ended on Sep 24, 2024.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A prolonged period of onshore flow combined with high astronomical tides resulted in multiple days of coastal flooding at DC Waterfront. The crest height was 5.62' MLLW and the time of crest was 1012 PM EST on Sep 21, 2024. Lead time for this event was 17.8 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A prolonged period of onshore flow combined with high astronomical tides resulted in multiple days of moderate coastal flooding at DC Waterfront. Moderate coastal flooding began on Sep 21 and ended on Sep 24, 2024.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A prolonged period of onshore flow combined with high astronomical tides resulted in multiple days of moderate coastal flooding. The crest height was 5.49' NAVD88 and the time of crest was 1212 AM EST. Lead time was 21.3 hours.
